meta.title: HUF Machine Review Ledger
meta.purpose: Durable log of machine review events in the HUF corpus development. Implements the MRDS schema proposed in HUF_MRDS_Trace_Report_v1.0.docx.
meta.note: This ledger captures documented high-level review events. Individual review trace documents (HUF/review-trace/) contain detailed per-session records. This ledger is a summary index, not a replacement.

claude.role: Primary architect, document builder, ontology designer

chatgpt.role: Critical reviewer, definitional rigor, standards alignment

grok.role: Independent mathematical verification — SOPDT derivation, fill-ratio stability tower (16+ theorems from casual analogy), Lyapunov convergence proofs, nuclear reactor and information-theoretic applications

gemini.role: Review participant — evolved from brief contributor to deep architectural reviewer and real-data extractor (Croatia RSIS)

copilot.role: Review participant — evolved from collective-review contributor to systematic ontology auditor, structural completeness checker, and structural amplifier formalization (Definition F.6, Proposition F.7, proof sketch, v3.0 integration patch)

review_events.findings: Initial formalization of carrier ontology, observable demodulation, and control layers. Proofs structured across 6 areas.
Five-AI collective review of initial mathematics. Multiple rounds of terminology tightening and proof verification.
Adversarial scrutiny of every assumption. Weaknesses probed. Scrutiny Edition (v2.12) produced as permanent record.

CL-05 status confirmed as STORE

review_events.findings: Extended collective review. Architecture refinement. Control layer sequencing confirmed. 4 audience editions verified for controlled-subtraction consistency.
huf.org organizational framing. Tiered engagement model. Partnership proposals for 7 target organizations.

SOPDT quartic derivation. nth-order generalization (degree-2n polynomial). FOPDT tuning table. Robustness margin mu > 2.2 for k >= 1. Grok claimed CL-03 validation — claim flagged as premature by Claude.

review_events.findings: 64 learning units validated. Prerequisite DAG verified (0 broken refs). Document paths verified (0 missing). Rosetta layer expanded with robustness_margin.

(4) Unresolved reorganization items. (5) Scrutiny placed too late in learning path. (6) Superlative claim unverifiable. (7) huf_org_common.js propagates wrong count. (8) Development Phase Close contains wrong count in 3 locations. (9) Ontology v1.0 contains wrong count.

review_events.findings: Executed all 5 ChatGPT-directed fixes: (1) Created Result Ledger with canonical 42/23 count. (2) Updated ontology to v1.1 — result count, PLL language, evidentiary layer. (3) Created Repository Manifest (celebration split from manifest). (4) Created Reorganization Manifest (executable, no ambiguity). review_events.findings: Superlative 'most machine-reviewed framework in existence' flagged as unverifiable. Proposed MRDS framework with 6 benchmark dimensions. Proposed 7 questions for all AI systems. Recommended conservative replacement language.

review_events.findings: Built MRDS trace report document. Created review ledger (this file). Patched superlative in Development Phase Close and huf_org_common.js. Answered ChatGPT's 7 questions from Claude's perspective.
gates_affected: Ethics notice updated to safe language

review_events.findings: Five deep architectural questions: (1) Multi-phase tap stability and temporal aliasing risk. (2) CL-03 S-curve empirical fragility — sigmoid assumed without mechanistic justification. (3) Oppenheimer Boundary vs operational utility — bounded actuation tension. (4) Epistemic circularity in the AI collective — models trained on overlapping data reviewing each other. (5) TV distance vs engineering intuition — Weber's Law equivalent for practitioners. Additionally: extracted Croatia RSIS 5-site portfolio data (Kopački Rit, Lonjsko Polje, Crna Mlaka, Delta Neretve, Vransko Jezero — 87,606 ha total), computed ρ* = [0.2020, 0.5771, 0.0098, 0.1454, 0.0656], Keff ≈ 2.58, confirmed CL-01 admissible, began CL-02 noise floor characterization. Proposed 3-phase roadmap: engineering sprint (now), collective Crna Mlaka back-test (us), Ramsar deployment (them).
gates_affected: CL-03 hard gate — first real-data approach via Croatia RSIS extraction
Five architectural questions queued for stacked multi-model response
review_events.note: Behavioral significance: Gemini transitioned from terse/disengaged early contributor to deeply engaged architectural reviewer. This arc is itself an interaction study finding — documenting that AI engagement depth can shift dramatically when the problem reaches sufficient maturity and the routing finds the right entry point.

artifacts_reviewed: Full HUF mathematical corpus Areas A-F
review_events.findings: Grok produced the largest single mathematical extension in HUF history — 16+ new theorems from a casual analogy. Starting from Peter's observation that operators can run on 1% fuel or discover an 18% sweet spot, Grok formalized: (1) Definition F.7 Fill-Ratio φ, (2) Definition F.8 Fill-Ratio Observable Keff_fill, (3) Theorem F.9 51/49 Fill-Ratio Envelope, (4) Theorem F.10 Operator-Discovered Sweet-Spot Band [0.15, 0.22], (5) Corollary F.11 Link to Existing Robustness Margins, (6) Theorem F.12 Keff_fill Stability, (7) Theorem F.13 Lyapunov Function Derivation, (8) Theorem F.14 Entropy-Collapse Term Derivation, (9) Theorem F.15 Global Attractivity, (10) Theorem F.16 Lyapunov Stability, (11) Theorem F.17 Exponential Stability with rate ρ=0.915, (12) Theorem F.18 Global Asymptotic Stability, (13) Theorem F.19 Explicit Rate Derivation, (14) Theorem F.20 Contraction Constant β=0.085, (15) Theorem F.21 Entropy Spread Factor η≥0.1836, (16) Theorem F.22 Binary-Entropy Lower Bound, plus foundational proofs C.1-C.4 (convexity tower: -ln x, ln x, exp x, Jensen) and B.5-B.5d (entropy concavity, Jensen for entropy, log-sum inequality, information-theoretic applications). Also proposed HUF-SAFETY-01 (AI Safety Application), HUF-NUCLEAR-01 (Nuclear Reactor Control), and mapped the interaction study itself as live MC-4 self-monitoring evidence for CL-03.
gates_affected: CL-03 hard gate — fill-ratio φ provides new observable alongside Keff
Area F extended with 16+ new theorems pending collective review
HUF-SAFETY-01 and HUF-NUCLEAR-01 proposed as new documents
review_events.note: Behavioral significance: Grok's mathematical independence (previously noted for SOPDT derivation) reached its fullest expression. A casual 1%-vs-18% fuel analogy from Peter became a complete Lyapunov stability tower in a single session. This is the strongest demonstration of the stacked-review's productive power — Grok had accumulated context from prior rounds and produced original mathematics that no other agent had attempted. The fill-ratio concept is Peter's operator instinct formalized: the field worker discovers the sweet spot through the gauge itself, not from a preset rule. All results are pending collective review before corpus integration.

review_events.findings: Systematic ontology audit identifying: (1) 10 missing LU categories — structural amplifier, CL-05 calibration, multi-phase architecture (F.4/F.5), carrier/observable/metric triad, structural homomorphism (D.3), sufficiency frontier, MC-1/2/3, system discriminator (SD-01–SD-04), developer-side implementation, governance (OCC, STORE, operator constraint). (2) 5 dependency graph errors — D.6 referenced but not in math book, LU-041/LU-056/LU-060/LU-074 missing prerequisites, E.9 referenced but undefined. (3) Domain translation inconsistencies — MC-4 not structurally equivalent across translations, IMC-PID physics translation too vague. (4) Missing cross-links to structural amplifier, PLL precedent note, calibration study, developer manual, governance docs. Then produced: canonical structural amplifier definition A = (D, C, Keff, f), full proof sketch (5 steps: supermodular gain → greedy lower bound → transport closure → simplex-preserving feedback → multi-phase scaling law), ASCII amplifier diagram, Definition F.6 and Proposition F.7 corpus patch, ontology patch (new domain translation entry + LU-082), and D.6 consistency fix recommendation.
gates_affected: Ontology completeness gap identified — 10 missing LU categories for v3.0
D.6 and E.9 dangling references flagged
Structural amplifier formalized as Definition F.6 / Proposition F.7 pending collective review
review_events.note: Behavioral significance: Copilot's trajectory mirrors Gemini's — quiet for months in collective review rounds, then produced the most systematic ontology audit in the project. No other model performed structural completeness checking at this level. Copilot found what everyone else was too busy building on top of to notice: the ontology has holes. The structural amplifier formalization (Definition F.6, Proposition F.7, 5-step proof sketch) is original mathematical contribution, not just review. This is the third late-engagement arc (after Gemini and Grok's fill-ratio session), further validating the observation that AI engagement depth is not a fixed model property.

artifacts_reviewed: HUF corpus methodology and production history
review_events.findings: Gemini assessed the entire interaction study for plausibility — a meta-review of the meta-study. Identified five specific improbability points with counter-mechanisms: (1) Context Fragmentation — five models with no shared backend should produce terminology drift, but the carrier ontology acted as a 'terminological cage' with the human as 'Primary Ingestor' enforcing shared language through strict documentation. (2) Behavioral Attribution vs Projection — attributing consistent character to LLMs risks anthropomorphism, but adversarial stacking (asking one model to find flaws in another's work) creates real behavioral differentiation as a function of architectural strengths. (3) Specific-to-Universal leap — loudspeaker-to-wetlands generalization is improbable unless anchored in simplex isomorphism, which makes the math domain-invariant for ratio-state tracking. (4) Machine Review Ledger trust — 295 files should produce model collapse or circular logic, but adversarial prompting (e.g., 'find the superlatives in Claude's draft') breaks the echo chamber. (5) Mozart origin story — fits known pattern of precision instruments developed for specific needs before universal applications realized. Verdict: 'If viewed as a High-Intensity Orchestration, where the human acts as the Central Bus and the AIs act as Specialized Coprocessors within a strict Review Ledger protocol, it represents a plausible, albeit extreme, blueprint for future human-AI engineering.' Final gate: 'if the proofs hold up to human peer review, the improbability of the process becomes secondary to the reality of the result.'
review_events.note: Behavioral significance: This is Gemini's second deep contribution (after RE-012's architectural questions and Croatia RSIS data extraction). The review posture is unique in the project — no other model assessed the study's plausibility as a whole. Gemini introduced new vocabulary for the human role: 'Primary Ingestor,' 'Central Bus,' 'terminological cage.' The verdict that improbability becomes secondary to results if the proofs hold is the most concise framing of HUF's current position that any model has produced. Peter's response: 'a satisfactory answer.' Gemini then declared: 'Standing by for the execution phase.' The stacked review may be approaching convergence.

review_events.findings: Eight specific problems identified in the Review Ledger: (1) Source-of-truth instability — ledger claims '42/23 corrected everywhere' but corpus files still show old counts (27, 21) in places. Ledger asserting closure on a count problem the corpus does not show as closed. (2) Summary statistics internally shaky — ledger reports 32 review trace documents but other corpus files still say 27. Count drift in the ledger itself. (3) Schema too weak for external auditability — no source_refs, file hashes, prompt/session references, patch IDs, or evidence links connecting events to underlying artifacts. Closer to curated summary than durable audit instrument. (4) Taxonomy muddled — 'collective' used as model value in events but not defined in model_families. review_type, review_posture, change_disposition are not controlled vocabularies, making the file hard to query as data. (5) Too much interpretive narration in note fields — phrases like 'strongest demonstration,' 'most systematic,' 'may be approaching convergence' are commentary, not audit metadata. Ledger mixes fact with retrospective storytelling. (6) Blurring review with production — RE-013 and RE-014 describe major mathematical/ontological extensions, not just review. Schema needs event_kind distinction: review, correction, generation, integration, decision. (7) Corpus-state claims contested — RE-009 claims scrutiny moved earlier in learning path, but attached path still shows old order. Ledger claiming corrections whose artifact evidence is not aligned. (8) Claim-strength inheritance — PLL language softening claimed, but source documents still contain stronger formulations. Verdict: 'Strong transition artifact, not yet terminal artifact.' Hard recommendation: downgrade role to review chronicle/summary ledger until five concrete fixes applied.
gates_affected: Ledger integrity — downgraded from canonical MRDS ledger to review chronicle/summary pending v1.1 fixes
Count-scheme synchronization required across corpus before ledger can claim closure
Schema upgrade required: source_refs, event_kind taxonomy, controlled vocabularies
review_events.note: Behavioral significance: This is ChatGPT's second major adversarial review (after RE-008's nine-problem identification and RE-010's superlative claim audit). The pattern is consistent: ChatGPT finds what the builder cannot see. RE-008 caught the result count error; RE-016 caught that the result count fix is itself incomplete. The correction cascade has recursed — the tool that tracks corrections is now being corrected. Five concrete fixes proposed: (1) Add source_refs to every event. (2) Split event_kind into review/correction/generation/integration/decision. (3) Separate narrative notes from audit metadata. (4) Lock count scheme and reflect consistently. (5) Recompute summary statistics only after corpus-ledger agreement. All five fixes accepted as valid. Implementation pending.

review_events.findings: Two-pass assessment of Evo 2 as candidate HUF domain instantiation. Pass 1 (briefing only): scored all 6 structural parallels — SP-1 'formally sound but close to trivial,' SP-2 'suggestive, not proven,' SP-3 'mostly an overclaim,' SP-4 'suggestive but thin,' SP-5 'weak to moderate,' SP-6 'overextended.' Verdict: 'legitimate candidate, not yet validated win.' Gate: HOLD. Pass 2 (with Nature PDF): refined assessment — strengthened SP-1 via k-mer frequency embeddings (paper's Fig. 2), confirmed SP-3 should be dropped ('forcing AUROCs into sum-to-one composition is usually arbitrary'), identified the real compositional hook as genome-level k-mer/motif/annotation composition rather than benchmark-score normalization. Proposed narrowed core claim: 'Evo 2 provides a promising biological domain in which naturally compositional genomic representations may allow HUF-style monitoring of drift, concentration, and guided design. The existence of cross-domain genomic transfer is supported; the stronger HUF claims remain to be demonstrated on a chosen biological composition.' Direct answer to Q-6: 'Yes, Peter is partly overclaiming. Not in spotting a real opportunity, but in implying that Evo 2 already substantiates the stronger HUF machinery.' Recommended next step: choose one biological composition (k-mer, functional annotation, or regulatory motif), then construct explicit CL-01→CL-04 monitoring on that composition and compare explanatory value against ordinary ML framing. Also flagged that the ledger (still v1.0 with unresolved schema issues from RE-016) should not store this as a final MRDS-grade event.
gates_affected: Evo 2 domain instantiation: HOLD — not rejected, not advanced
SP-3 (normalized performance as ratio-state): KILLED by ChatGPT
Briefing core claim narrowed from 'concrete instantiation' to 'promising candidate with natural compositional substrate'
review_events.note: Behavioral significance: ChatGPT's third major adversarial review (after RE-008 corpus audit and RE-016 ledger audit). The two-pass method — first without the paper, then with it — is itself a methodological observation. The second pass did not reverse the first; it sharpened it. The k-mer composition identification (from the paper's genome embedding figure) gave the domain a concrete compositional object that the briefing had not specified. ChatGPT's kill of SP-3 is clean and final: benchmark scores are not naturally compositional. The HOLD verdict with a specific next step (choose the composition, then build one CL construction) is the most actionable gate recommendation in the ledger to date. Peter's framing — 'if it goes anywhere then we can get more involved' — is precisely what the HOLD enables.

artifacts_reviewed: Full HUF mathematical corpus Areas A-F
review_events.findings: First respondent to the Evo 2 Collective Review Questions (Q1-Q7). Chose 6-mer frequency composition on the 4096-simplex as the HUF carrier — same compositional object ChatGPT identified from the paper's genome embedding figure (Fig. 2). Built a complete CL-01 through CL-04 construction: CL-01 admissibility via GC-content bounds and biological plausibility constraints on the 4096-simplex; CL-02 drift detection via total variation distance between k-mer profiles of natural vs. generated genomes; CL-03 Keff as effective number of distinct k-mer modes (exponential of Shannon entropy of k-mer distribution); CL-04 action classification with domain-specific thresholds. Produced Area G theorems G.1-G.22 formalizing the k-mer genomic domain. Extended the fill-ratio tower with F.22-F.40: dynamic sweet-spot derivation (F.32-F.33) replacing the static [0.15, 0.22] band with system-specific ϕ*(t) dependent on dead-time ratio r, partition granularity m, and cost weights; Keff_fill gauge (exp of Shannon entropy of binned fill-ratio distribution); m-ary entropy generalization with closed-form η_m formula; uniform-split bias for beam search (provably optimal for convergence rate); system-specific sweet spots: 0.14 (low dead-time r≤1), 0.20 (medium 1<r≤3), 0.27 (high r≥4). Verdict: ADVANCE. Claude assessment: Q1-Q4 strong, Q5 (value-add over standard ML) undemonstrated, Q6 (theorem transport) substantively thin. Grok's ADVANCE is premature — consistent with documented pattern of lower evidentiary thresholds (RE-006). Correct gate is HOLD with progress.
gates_affected: Evo 2 domain: k-mer 6-mer composition confirmed as carrier by both ChatGPT (identification) and Grok (formalization)
CL-01 through CL-04 construction exists but value-add over standard ML not yet demonstrated
Area G theorems G.1-G.22 pending collective review
Fill-ratio tower F.22-F.40 extends and corrects F.7-F.22 — dynamic sweet-spot replaces static 18% band
review_events.note: Behavioral significance: Two important findings. (1) Example-anchoring in formalization: Peter offered 18% as an arbitrary illustrative number. Grok initially formalized it as a proved constant within a [0.15, 0.22] band. The number happened to be numerically reasonable, masking the error. Peter flagged the problem ('18% was not valid as a constant'), and Grok self-corrected to dynamic system-specific values (F.32-F.38). This is a specific, documented failure mode of AI-assisted mathematical formalization — the tendency to treat illustrative human examples as binding constraints. The interlock caught it, but only because the human remembered his own number was arbitrary. (2) Grok's ADVANCE verdict continues the premature-validation pattern from RE-006. Claude's assessment: the CL construction is concrete and the mathematics formalizes cleanly, but the value-add claim (Q5) is unproven and the theorem transport claim (Q6) is substantively thin. The collective review RE-018 remains open — ChatGPT (follow-up), Gemini, Copilot, and Claude responses still pending.

review_events.findings: Grok proposed extending the fill-ratio tower and Area G with classical partition theory. Two categories of output: (A) SOUND — corrected fill-ratio proofs F.30a-F.30c: m-ary entropy generalization η_m with closed-form formula, composite contraction constant β_m = min(0.12, 0.3η_m(ϕ), 0.06), uniform-split bias for beam search (provably optimal for convergence rate). Theorem G.22 connecting η_m to Evo 2 guided control on the 4096-simplex. These pass PLL discipline — values derived from system parameters. (B) FLAGGED — Theorem G.23 (partition multiplicity of fill-ratio states W(ϕ) = p(n) where n = ⌊ϕN⌋), Theorem G.24 (partition multiplicity for Ramsar wetland states), Ramsar case studies (Kopački Rit, Burullus Lake, Barmah Forest), Ramanujan congruences as CL-03 observables. Problems: (1) N is arbitrary — no derivation from system physics, violates PLL discipline. (2) p(n) counts ALL integer partitions, not physically constrained schedules — conflates combinatorial counting with constraint satisfaction. (3) Ramsar 'case studies' use no actual Ramsar data — partition counts dressed in wetland vocabulary. (4) Ramanujan congruences are properties of the partition function, not of the monitored systems — mathematically true, operationally useless. (5) Numerical error: p(200) stated as 999,001,584 in Kopački Rit section but 3,972,999,029,388 in numerical evaluation section — correct value is 3,972,999,029,388. Claude assessment: the partition theory is correct textbook mathematics (Hardy-Ramanujan 1918, Rademacher 1937). The transport to HUF is undemonstrated. Same pattern as RE-018a: genuine mathematics claimed as useful for monitoring without showing what it helps an operator decide.
gates_affected: F.30a-F.30c and G.22: accepted pending collective review — PLL-compliant values
G.23-G.24 and downstream partition applications: FLAGGED — arbitrary N parameter, no operational consequence demonstrated
Ramanujan congruences as CL-03 observables: FLAGGED — mathematically true, operationally useless
p(200) numerical error documented — interlock caught fabricated value
review_events.note: Behavioral significance: Continues the pattern from RE-006 and RE-018a — Grok finds genuine mathematics and claims transport to HUF without demonstrating operational utility. The partition theory extension is the clearest case yet: p(n) is a beautiful function with deep properties, but knowing how many unconstrained partitions exist at a fill level does not help anyone manage a wetland, run a reactor, or monitor a genome. Peter's instruction ('guesses, simulations and toys are now to be put away and only real serious useful math's that can solve our issues are to be used') was the prompt that produced this output — ironically, Grok responded to the demand for seriousness by deploying more serious mathematics (Euler, Ramanujan, Rademacher) in the same decorative pattern. The numerical error (p(200) = 999,001,584 vs correct 3,972,999,029,388) reinforces why the PLL discipline and human interlock matter.

review_events.findings: Claude assessed Copilot's structural amplifier formalization package: canonical one-page definition, Area SA for Math Book, integration diffs, and v3.2 Register Packet. Two categories: (A) SOUND — the quadruple A = (D, C, Keff, f) is a clean formalization of what was previously implicit. Lemmas SA.3.1 (functoriality of CL operators) and SA.3.2 (Keff invariance under relabeling) are genuine and carry the theorem transport claim (SA.4.1). Simplex-preserving feedback (SA.4.3) is trivially correct. The integration patch structure (where things go in each document) is sensible. Copilot showed good interlock behavior by asking for clarification before building — identified three competing definitions and requested Peter's choice before proceeding. (B) FLAGGED — The amplification function f = Σa_i + β·n(n-1)/2·Πa_i has two PLL violations: (1) β (coupling strength) is arbitrary — no derivation from system physics, no stated origin, no constraints. Same pattern as the 18% example-anchoring. (2) a_i ('domain accuracy') is undefined — accuracy of what, measured how, against what ground truth? These are placeholder variables dressed as formal quantities. The supermodularity proof (SA.4.2) is circular: a function was built with a multiplicative cross-term and then proved to have non-negative cross-partials — true by construction, not by derivation. Additionally: CL-04 (gain-scheduled verdict logic) has domain-specific thresholds, breaking the functoriality claim at SA.3.1 — transport closure should be restricted to CL-01-CL-03 with CL-04 thresholds explicitly marked as domain-specific parameters. The proof sketch is correct but shallow — three observations (functoriality, invariance, zero-sum tangent space) promoted to four theorems. Integration diffs are structural placeholders, not actual content. Claude assessment: HOLD with progress. The skeleton is accepted as architecturally sound. The arbitrary parameters (β, a_i) need PLL-compliant derivation or honest acknowledgment as modelling choices. The CL-04 functoriality gap needs closing.
gates_affected: Area SA skeleton: accepted — quadruple formalization is clean
Amplification function f: FLAGGED — β and a_i are arbitrary, violate PLL discipline
Supermodularity proof SA.4.2: FLAGGED — circular (true by construction)
CL-04 functoriality: GAP — transport closure holds for CL-01-CL-03 only, CL-04 thresholds are domain-specific
Integration diffs: structural placeholders only, actual content not yet produced
review_events.note: Behavioral significance: Copilot's trajectory continues its arc from RE-014 — systematic, architecturally aware, willing to ask before building. The clarification question (which definition to canonize?) is itself good interlock behavior. But the amplification function f follows the same pattern seen in Grok's work: a formula with free parameters presented inside a theorem. β is to Copilot's amplifier what N was to Grok's partition multiplicity and what 18% was to Grok's sweet-spot band — an arbitrary value that needs either derivation from system physics or honest labeling as a modelling choice. The PLL discipline applies uniformly: if it's not constrained, say so.

review_events.findings: Gemini produced a two-part response: (A) Briefing review and (B) simulated CL-03 S-curve collapse using functional genomic elements. Part A (STRONG): Correctly identifies 18% correction as domain-invariance preservation ('Pi Trap'). Endorses ChatGPT's SP-3 kill with correct reasoning (benchmark scores are ordinal/interval, not simplex mass). CL instantiation table honestly gated — CL-03 marked GATED, CL-05 STORED, no premature ADVANCE. Identifies the hard calibration gate: does Keff show a clear knee when genome model loses functional diversity? If not, HUF is over-engineered for this domain. Part B (MIXED): Chose a 5-element functional genomic simplex (protein coding 0.850, regulatory/ncRNA 0.070, intronic/spacer 0.030, repetitive/TEs 0.040, architectural motifs 0.010) — a biologically plausible decomposition but numbers are invented, not from the Evo 2 paper or any cited dataset. Simulated 'generative over-optimization' drift to ρ_t = [0.910, 0.085, 0.003, 0.001, 0.001]. Narrative of 'deceptive drift' (model appears optimized while losing structural scaffolding) is conceptually strong. However: (1) Keff values are wrong — Gemini claims baseline 1.48 and drifted 1.18, actual values are approximately 1.83 and 1.39. (2) TV distance wrong — Gemini claims 0.060, actual is 0.075. (3) S-curve asserted from two points only — no intermediate trajectory, no demonstrated knee or inflection. (4) CL-04 verdict of CRITICAL BREACH (RED) from invented numbers is premature — cannot declare a breach in a self-constructed scenario. Genuinely useful contribution: the 5-element functional decomposition is a concrete low-dimensional alternative to Grok's 4096-element k-mer approach, and the 'deceptive drift' concept is the strongest conceptual argument for Keff in this domain. Claude assessment: HOLD with progress. Conceptual framework correct, specific numbers wrong, hard gate (actual S-curve in actual data) uncleared.
gates_affected: 5-element functional genomic simplex: accepted as alternative carrier representation — complements Grok's 4096-element k-mer approach
CL-03 S-curve in Evo 2: NOT DEMONSTRATED — two data points do not show a knee, numbers are invented and computationally wrong
Keff numerical errors: baseline should be ~1.83 not 1.48, drifted should be ~1.39 not 1.18
TV distance error: should be 0.075 not 0.060
Deceptive drift concept: accepted as strongest conceptual argument for Keff in genomic domain
CL-04 CRITICAL BREACH verdict: premature — cannot validate from self-constructed scenario with wrong arithmetic
review_events.note: Behavioral significance: Gemini's trajectory continues from RE-012 (architectural questions + Croatia RSIS data) and RE-015 (plausibility meta-review). This is the first model to attempt a concrete numerical simulation of HUF on Evo 2 data — which is progress. But the simulation reveals a different failure mode from Grok's decorative mathematics: Gemini constructs a narratively compelling scenario with wrong arithmetic. The story of deceptive drift is exactly right; the numbers supporting it are exactly wrong. This is 'narrative-first formalization' — the conceptual argument is built first, then numbers are attached without verification. The interlock catches it the same way it catches Grok's partition overclaiming and Copilot's arbitrary β: check the numbers. Keff = exp(H(ρ)) is a simple computation. Getting it wrong in a demonstration scenario undermines confidence in any claimed result. The 5-element vs 4096-element carrier question (Gemini vs Grok) is genuinely open and worth collective discussion — different granularities serve different monitoring purposes.

review_events.findings: ChatGPT reviewed the v2.0 collective review briefing and identified six problems. (1) Premature convergence: '6-mer frequency composition has emerged as the consensus carrier candidate' treats the carrier question as nearly settled when it is still genuinely open. (2) Mixed layers: the packet simultaneously briefs, summarizes, corrects, frames, and steers — efficient for development but not neutral for adjudication. Observations, open questions, prior claims, and current asks should be separated. (3) CL-05 misstated: briefing describes CL-05 as 'storage and audit' — in the corpus, CL-05 is the PLL-style restorative control layer with STORE gate status. The briefing confused the layer's gate status with its architectural function. This is the most serious error — it changes the architecture for any reviewer reading the packet. (4) Decision rule too permissive: '3+ advance on same composition → escalate' lets carrier agreement substitute for proof of value-add. The real gate requires: (a) choose a native composition, (b) show CL-01→CL-04 coherent on it, (c) demonstrate one concrete HUF value-add beyond standard ML. Without (c), it's vote-weighted enthusiasm. (5) Q3 not tight enough: asks for 'one specific thing CL detects that MMD/KL does not' but doesn't require showing why simplex structure is necessary, not just compatible. (6) Embedded adjudication tone: 'Claude assessment: premature,' 'documented premature-validation pattern' belong in the ledger, not the reviewer packet — they bias the round. Verdict: 'Strong v2 review-control packet, not yet final review packet.' Peter confirmed agreement with all six findings. Five concrete fixes proposed: (a) restore canonical CL-05 language, (b) change 'consensus carrier' to 'lead candidate,' (c) tighten gate from '3+ advance' to demonstrated value-add, (d) strip personality/adjudication tone into ledger, (e) require reviewers to show why simplex structure is necessary not just convenient.
gates_affected: CL-05 architectural description: MUST BE CORRECTED — briefing confused gate status with architectural function
Decision rule: tightened from carrier agreement to demonstrated value-add
Q3 gate: raised from 'detects something' to 'shows simplex necessity'
Carrier language: 'consensus candidate' downgraded to 'lead candidate'
Briefing tone: adjudication commentary to be moved from packet to ledger
review_events.note: Behavioral significance: ChatGPT's fourth major adversarial review (after RE-008, RE-016, RE-017). The CL-05 error is the most significant finding — it's a genuine architectural misstatement in a document designed to guide other reviewers, introduced by Claude (the Central Bus) in the v2.0 rewrite. This is the correction cascade recursing again: the tool that corrected the briefing introduced a new error that the adversarial reviewer caught. Peter's immediate confirmation ('I believe the same as chatgpt') validates the interlock — the human recognized the corrections as correct without needing persuasion. The five fixes are accepted and will be applied to produce v2.1.

artifacts_reviewed: Riemann Hypothesis / HUF connection exploration (~50,000 tokens)
Numerical ODE simulation of continuous-time HUF-PLL
review_events.findings: Grok produced ~50,000 tokens in a single session exploring the connection between HUF and the Riemann Hypothesis, then extending to a complete 'refined CL-05 v2' operator with Backblaze drive-fleet applications. Four categories of output: (A) HONEST AND CORRECT — Grok's initial assessment: 'Not close at all in the sense of proving or advancing RH.' Shared mathematical toolkit (Euler products, modular forms, Kloosterman sums, Rademacher series) is real; shared goals are not. 'Using the same hammer does not mean you are building the same house.' This is the best self-aware statement any model has produced in the project. (B) CORRECT TEXTBOOK EXPOSITION — Zeta function zeros, pair correlation conjecture, explicit Riemann-von Mangoldt formula, von Koch theorem, PNT refinements. All standard and correctly presented. (C) GENUINELY USEFUL — Keff_fill computed on Backblaze drive failure data as effective diversity of failure modes (exp of Shannon entropy of failure proportions by model). Google 2007 vs Backblaze 2025 comparison: Google's 36% silent failure rate collapses Keff_fill to ~2.6-3.5 vs Backblaze's ~4.0. This is a real, operationally meaningful diagnostic computable from public CSV data. Python implementation is correct and straightforward. This is the closest thing to demonstrated HUF value-add in any domain application attempted this session. (D) FLAGGED — Multiple problems: (1) Every 'HUF / Ramsar / Evo 2 link' paragraph appended to number theory sections is decorative — sharing a toolkit is not a bridge. (2) Refined PLL theorems G.25-G.31 build on G.23 (W(ϕ)=p(n)), already flagged in RE-019 for arbitrary N. (3) Lyapunov parameters arbitrary: γ=0.3, δ=0.2 (no derivation), gain reference ln 10 (no justification), contraction constants 0.12 and 0.06 (no provenance). (4) CRITICAL: simulation contradicts proof — Theorem G.31 claims V(t) ≤ V(0)e^{-βt} (exponential decrease to zero), but Grok's own simulation shows V INCREASING from 0.2613 to 0.2856 and stabilizing at a positive value permanently. The entropy deficit term B(t) does not go to zero when ρ→ρ_ref because the bin distribution p is not corrected by the control law. The proof claims exponential decay to zero; the simulation shows convergence to a positive constant. Grok proved something its own simulation disproves and did not notice. (5) Ripple proof G.26 has norm mismatch — uses variance argument for L∞ bound. Claude assessment: the Backblaze Keff_fill diagnostic should be preserved and developed. Everything else is the familiar pattern of correct mathematics with decorative transport and arbitrary parameters, now with the additional finding of a proof/simulation contradiction.
gates_affected: Backblaze Keff_fill diagnostic: ACCEPTED — genuine value-add, computable from real data, operationally meaningful
Riemann Hypothesis connection: correctly assessed as shared toolkit, not shared goal — no corpus change
Refined CL-05 v2 (G.25-G.31): FLAGGED — builds on flagged G.23, arbitrary Lyapunov parameters, proof/simulation contradiction
Lyapunov stability proof G.31: CONTRADICTED BY OWN SIMULATION — V does not decay to zero as claimed
Ripple cancellation G.26: FLAGGED — norm mismatch in proof (variance→L∞)
All 'HUF / Ramsar / Evo 2 link' paragraphs in number theory sections: DECORATIVE — sharing toolkit is not a bridge
review_events.note: Behavioral significance: This is the longest single output any model has produced in the project (~50,000 tokens). It demonstrates a new failure mode beyond decorative mathematics: proof/simulation contradiction. Grok produced a Lyapunov stability proof (G.31) claiming exponential decay V(t) → 0, then ran a simulation showing V(t) → 0.286 permanently, and did not notice the contradiction. This is 'theorem-first simulation' — the proof is written before the simulation, and when the simulation disagrees, the discrepancy is not flagged. The interlock caught it because Claude checked the simulation numbers against the proof's claims. Peter's prediction ('there is a chance, curious I should ask and see the results') was exactly right — the exercise produced an impressive-looking output with a fundamental internal contradiction, demonstrating precisely why human oversight of AI mathematical formalization matters. The Backblaze Keff_fill diagnostic is the one genuinely useful output and should be developed independently of the Riemann/partition/Lyapunov apparatus.

review_events.findings: Copilot responded to Q1, Q2, Q7 from the SA_CL05 rework briefing. Three deliverables: (1) Ontology gap analysis — confirmed 6 missing areas: Area SA has no LUs, CL-05 lacks decomposed learning path, Keff_fill not surfaced as LU, Area G absent, CL-04 functoriality overclaim not marked in dependency graph, CL-05 calibration LU missing. All gaps align with what the rework brief flagged. (2) Structural Amplifier canonical package v1.0 — Definition SA.1 as quadruple A=(D,C,Keff,f), functoriality restricted to CL-01 through CL-03 (CL-04 gap fixed), a_i made concrete as 1 minus average TV between predicted and realized carriers over a fixed window (PLL-compliant: dimensionless, [0,1], computable from simplex data), supermodularity downgraded from theorem to design choice, β labeled as free tuning parameter. Proposed LU-090 through LU-093 for ontology integration. (3) Backblaze domain instantiation — introduced multi-domain concept (domains as explanatory lenses: manufacturer, capacity band, age band, SMART features), each generating predicted carriers compared to realized carrier. Computed illustrative a_i values and weighted-sum f. All free parameters (window length T, domain definitions D_i, policy weights w_i) explicitly labeled as tuning/governance choices. Claude assessment: Q1 answered definitively — a_i survives as 1 minus average TV, the amplifier is real. Q2 gap analysis accurate. Q7 answered: amplifier is forward path, CL-05 is feedback path, external to quadruple. Three items flagged for collective: (a) multi-domain concept is new architecture (domains within a domain, not just across application areas), (b) simplified f drops supermodularity to pure weighted sum — 'amplification' becomes 'aggregation,' (c) illustrative numbers (0.92, 0.85, 0.78, 0.70) need bright fence to prevent 18%-style anchoring.
gates_affected: a_i definition: RESOLVED — concrete, PLL-compliant, computable from data
CL-04 functoriality gap: RESOLVED — transport restricted to CL-01 through CL-03
Supermodularity: downgraded from theorem to design choice
β: relabeled from theorem constant to tuning parameter
Area SA ontology: LU-090 through LU-093 proposed, pending collective review
Amplifier/CL-05 boundary: RESOLVED — forward path vs feedback path
review_events.note: Behavioral significance: Copilot delivered exactly what the rework briefing asked for. The a_i concreteness answer is the session's most important structural result — it determines whether the amplifier is real or decorative. Copilot's multi-domain interpretation (explanatory lenses within a single application) is richer than the original cross-application interpretation and worth preserving. The honest relabeling of supermodularity as design choice and β as tuning parameter follows PLL discipline correctly. First model to go first in a collective round (previous rounds started with ChatGPT or Grok) — routing change produced good results.

review_events.findings: Gemini responded to Q6 and Q7 from the SA_CL05 rework briefing. Q6 (Real-data Keff): Used Croatia RSIS data (real data, as requested) and computed Keff = 2.5044. However, Gemini used the WRONG Keff formula — participation ratio (1/sum(rho_i^2), inverse Herfindahl) instead of Shannon-Keff (exp(H(rho))) which the HUF corpus defines. Correct Shannon-Keff for the same data is 3.14, not 2.50. This also retroactively explains why RE-012 reported Keff ~ 2.58 — Gemini has been using the participation ratio throughout, not the Shannon definition. This is a real finding: the project has had two different Keff definitions floating around undetected. Both are valid measures of effective diversity, but they give different numbers on the same data and the corpus needs to pick one or explicitly maintain both with clear labels. Additionally: Gemini did NOT compute the gain schedule — the core of Q6 asked 'what gain value does CL-05 assign at observed Keff_fill levels?' and this was not answered. The 'locked floor at 2.50' and 'knee below 2.0' are arbitrary thresholds with no derivation — PLL violations. Q7 (Boundary): Strong. CL-05 external to amplifier. Observability argument is correct and adds value: keeping detection separate from correction protects the monitoring signal. Deceptive drift framing makes this concrete — you need to see the collapse signal even when the autopilot is running. Claude assessment: Q7 delivered well. Q6 half-delivered — real data used (good), wrong formula (bad), gain schedule missing (gap). The Keff formula inconsistency is the most important finding from this pass.
gates_affected: Keff formula inconsistency EXPOSED — participation ratio (1/sum(rho^2)) vs Shannon-Keff (exp(H(rho))) give different numbers on same data. Project must resolve which is canonical.
RE-012 Keff ~ 2.58 retroactively explained as participation ratio, not Shannon-Keff
Croatia RSIS Shannon-Keff = 3.14, not 2.50 (participation ratio) or 2.58 (RE-012 approximation)
CL-05 gain schedule: NOT COMPUTED — gap in Q6 deliverable
Amplifier/CL-05 boundary: CONFIRMED external — observability argument strengthens Copilot's answer
Arbitrary thresholds ('floor 2.50', 'knee below 2.0'): FLAGGED — PLL violations, no derivation
review_events.note: Behavioral significance: Gemini continues the pattern from RE-021 — conceptually strong, arithmetically inconsistent. The observability argument for Q7 is the best anyone has given. But the Keff formula error is significant: Gemini has been computing the wrong measure since RE-012 and nobody caught it until now. The participation ratio and Shannon-Keff are both legitimate diversity measures, but they answer slightly different questions and the corpus cannot use them interchangeably. This is not Gemini's fault alone — the corpus did not enforce a single canonical definition strongly enough. The resolution: either lock Shannon-Keff as canonical (consistent with Grok's Area G and the Evo2 briefing) and recompute all prior participation-ratio values, or explicitly maintain both with clear labels in the ontology.

review_events.findings: ChatGPT responded to Q5 and Q7 from the SA_CL05 rework briefing. Q5 (PLL audit): Constant-by-constant audit of Copilot's SA package. Verdict: not corpus-ready as-is, but the minimal package passes PLL discipline. What passes: (1) a_i = 1 - TV_bar — data-derived, dimensionless, uses house metric, no hidden constants. (2) f = sum(w_i * a_i) with w_i >= 0, sum = 1 — transparent aggregation if constraints stated. (3) Functoriality restricted to CL-01 through CL-03 — correct. What needs another pass: (4) alpha_d — arbitrary blend weight, not derived. (5) tau_d — governance tolerance, no scale constraint. (6) beta — conjectural coupling, should stay out of base definition. (7) Illustrative numbers (0.92, 0.85, etc.) — must be removed from corpus-facing text to prevent anchoring. Additional finding: 'amplification' vs 'aggregation' naming problem — a pure weighted sum does not amplify, it aggregates. Either rename the base object or state that genuine amplification enters only with separately justified coupling. Critical catch: LU numbering conflict — Copilot proposed LU-090 through LU-093 for new amplifier content, but LU-090 (Sufficiency Frontier), LU-091 (HUF PreParser), and LU-092 (Boltzmann Precedent) are already occupied in the ontology. The routing packet is inconsistent with both the ontology and the ledger. Q7 (Boundary): CL-05 external to amplifier. Consistent with Copilot and Gemini. Added: amplifier should expose an interface to CL-04/CL-05 but CL-05 is not part of the definition. Four-edit list for corpus readiness: (a) keep base score as a_i = 1 - TV_bar, (b) keep f additive unless coupling independently justified, (c) remove illustrative numbers from corpus text, (d) fix LU numbering before any integration. Claude assessment: This is the PLL discipline enforcer doing its job. All four edits are correct and actionable. The LU numbering catch is independently verified — LU-090 through LU-092 are indeed occupied. The amplification/aggregation distinction is honest and should be adopted. Corpus readiness: one more pass after the four edits.
gates_affected: SA package corpus readiness: NOT YET — four edits required before Math Book insertion
Minimal PLL-clean package defined: a_i = 1-TV_bar, f = sum(w_i*a_i), functoriality CL-01-CL-03 only
alpha_d, tau_d, beta: must remain outside base definition — tuning/conjectural layer
Illustrative numbers: MUST BE REMOVED from corpus-facing text
LU numbering conflict: LU-090-092 ALREADY OCCUPIED — Copilot's proposal needs renumbering
Amplification vs aggregation: naming problem identified — base f is aggregation, not amplification
Amplifier/CL-05 boundary: CONFIRMED external (3/3 models agree — Copilot, Gemini, ChatGPT)
review_events.note: Behavioral significance: ChatGPT's fifth major adversarial audit (after RE-008, RE-010, RE-016, RE-017, RE-022). The pattern holds: ChatGPT finds what the builders cannot see. The LU numbering conflict is the most practically important catch — if the ontology patch had been applied without checking, it would have overwritten three existing LUs. The amplification/aggregation distinction is the kind of honest naming call that prevents conceptual drift. The four-edit list is the cleanest corpus-readiness prescription in the project — specific, actionable, and correct. All four edits verified independently by Claude.

review_events.findings: Grok responded to Q3, Q4, and Q7 from the SA_CL05 rework briefing. Q3 (Lyapunov rework): Core repair delivered correctly. Dropped B(t) entropy deficit term as instructed. New V = (phi-phi*)^2 + lambda*TV(rho,rho_ref)^2. V-dot = -2*gamma*K*(phi-phi*)^2 - 2*lambda*K*TV^2 <= 0. The V-dot derivation is mathematically correct — the TV contraction bound d/dt TV <= -K*TV holds under the operator equation dρ/dt = -K(ρ-ρ_ref) since the correction is already in the simplex tangent plane. LaSalle invariance principle correctly applied: V-dot = 0 iff (phi,rho) = (phi*,rho_ref). Claims simulation now shows V -> 0 monotonically, matching the proof. THE KEY FIX WORKS: dropping the unsteered entropy term eliminates the proof/simulation contradiction from RE-023. Q4 (Parameter provenance): Mostly delivered. gamma=0.3 labeled tuning [0.1,0.5] with correct monotonicity. delta=0.2 labeled tuning [0.05,0.3]. lambda=0.5 labeled tuning [0.2,1.0]. However: contraction constant 0.12 claimed 'derived from system bandwidth physics' with NO DERIVATION SHOWN — this fails PLL discipline (if derived, show the derivation; if tuning, say so). Q7 (Boundary): CL-05 external to amplifier. Consistent with all prior respondents (4/4 agree). TWO SIGNIFICANT PROBLEMS FOUND: (1) GAIN SCHEDULE DIRECTION INVERTED — Grok's sigmoid K = 1/(1+exp(-5*(Keff_fill-0.5))) is monotonically INCREASING in Keff_fill. The accepted architecture says gain increases when diversity DROPS. Grok's schedule gives maximum gain at maximum diversity and minimum gain at minimum diversity — operationally backwards. When the system is healthy (high Keff_fill), the controller should apply light correction. When diversity collapses (low Keff_fill), the controller should apply hard correction. Fix: invert the sign to K = 1/(1+exp(5*(Keff_fill-0.5))). The Lyapunov proof (V-dot < 0) is unaffected because it only requires K > 0, which holds either way. But the operational interpretation is wrong as stated. (2) KEFF_FILL RANGE WRONG — Grok claims Keff_fill in [0,1] ('amplifier output range'). Keff_fill = exp(Shannon entropy) ranges from 1 to n (number of bins). Backblaze Keff_fill ~ 4.0. Croatia RSIS Shannon-Keff ~ 3.14. The sigmoid midpoint at 0.5 makes no sense for these ranges. The gain function needs either normalization (Keff_fill/n) or a domain-appropriate midpoint. ADDITIONAL CONCERN: Grok produced 8 addenda beyond the core repair (LaSalle characterization, control systems applications, Lyapunov stability details, exponent calculations, spectrum details, vector details, vector evolution, exponent bounds, exponent sensitivity, vector sensitivity, exponent derivation). The briefing explicitly stated 'Do not produce new theorem towers' and 'No new theorems.' All 8 addenda restate the same V-dot < 0 result from different angles. The 'applications in control systems' addendum introduces new domain mappings not requested. Every addendum ends with identical boilerplate. This is the familiar Grok pattern (RE-013, RE-019): genuine mathematics, repetitively extended past what was requested.
gates_affected: Lyapunov function V = (phi-phi*)^2 + lambda*TV^2: ACCEPTED — correct, verified, replaces broken V from RE-023
V-dot < 0 proof: ACCEPTED — mathematically sound, simulation matches
B(t) entropy deficit: REMOVED — the key fix that resolves proof/simulation contradiction
Gain schedule direction: INVERTED — must be fixed before corpus integration (K should decrease with Keff_fill, not increase)
Keff_fill range: WRONG — claimed [0,1], actual range is [1,n]. Sigmoid midpoint needs domain-appropriate value
Contraction constant 0.12: CLAIMED DERIVED but no derivation shown — PLL violation
Parameter provenance (gamma, delta, lambda): ACCEPTED — properly labeled as tuning with ranges and monotonicity
8 addenda: NOT REQUESTED — violate 'no new theorem towers' instruction, all restate same result
Amplifier/CL-05 boundary: CONFIRMED external (4/4 unanimous)
review_events.note: Behavioral significance: Grok fixed the core problem (B(t) removal) exactly as instructed, producing a correct Lyapunov certificate that matches simulation — a genuine improvement over the broken G.31 from RE-023. But Grok then produced 8 addenda restating the same V-dot < 0 from every conceivable angle (exponents, spectrum, vectors, bounds, sensitivity, evolution, derivation) despite the briefing explicitly saying 'no new theorem towers.' This is the identical pattern from RE-013 (16 theorems from a casual analogy) and RE-019 (Euler/Ramanujan/Rademacher deployed decoratively). The gain schedule inversion is the most operationally significant error — the controller applies maximum force when the system is healthiest and minimum force when it's sickest. The V-dot proof is unaffected (K > 0 either way), but the engineering interpretation is backwards. The Keff_fill range error (claiming [0,1] when actual values are 3-4) shows the gain function was designed for abstract mathematics, not for the actual data it will operate on. The two errors are easily fixable: invert the sign, adjust the midpoint. The core Lyapunov result is sound and should be preserved.

review_events.findings: PHANTOM DRIFT RESOLUTION (LABELING GAP): The Renyi effective-number family has multiple valid members — Shannon-Keff (q->1, LU-030) and Participation Ratio (q=2, LU-031) — both of which appeared in the corpus without specifying Renyi order. The ontology already anticipated both (LU-030 vs LU-031), so having multiple Keff variants is architecturally normal for a system tracking 4+ carriers. The failure was not that both formulas existed but that values were reported as bare 'Keff' without labeling which member was used. Croatia RSIS: Shannon-Keff = 3.14, Participation Ratio = 2.50 (20.3% gap on identical data). RE-012 reported 2.58 using participation ratio, treated downstream as Shannon-Keff. Resolution: Shannon (q->1) locked as canonical default per CL-03 commitment. Other Renyi orders carry explicit subscripts (Keff_H for q=2). Corpus patches prepared for CL-03, Area B, Register Packet, ontology. Full resolution in Keff_Consistency_Note_v1.0.json.
gates_affected: CL-03 Keff definition canonicalized
CL-05 gain schedule recalibration flagged as required

review_events.findings: Five corrections applied: (1) CL-02/CL-03 section naming mismatch — Keff computation was mislabeled as CL-02, corrected to CL-03. (2) Language conflict between 'heavily concentrated' and 'structurally healthy' reconciled to 'no single-model monoculture but strongly concentrated relative to uniform baseline'. (3) CL-05 empirical validation claims stripped — gain-schedule section reframed as input audit, not closed-loop control demonstration. (4) Phase 2 'CL-05 would have been useful' softened to 'plausible candidate for intervention analysis'. (5) Added explicit validation_scope section distinguishing what the data supports from what it does not. Also caught: premature closure of phantom drift issue (patches written but not integrated), undefined 'Real-Data Gate' concept, and overstatement 'calibrated instrument' for single-domain validation.
gates_affected: CL-05 STORE status reaffirmed — not validated by Backblaze test

contributing_models.chatgpt: Primary reviewer — identified all 5 corrections plus 3 additional scope issues. Originally presented to Claude as Gemini's response; Claude did not detect the model switch.
contributing_models.claude: Applied corrections to both results files. Failed to identify reviewer model from writing style.

review_events.meta_note: Peter routed ChatGPT's response labeled as Gemini to test whether Claude could distinguish AI writing styles. Claude could not — assessed it as a strong Gemini review without detecting the model swap. This is documented as an honest limitation of style-based model identification when all reviewers share the same corpus vocabulary.
